rows.rationale: All 217 service rows are represented as pass in the current requirement ledger.
The exact row-level evidence gap report is empty.
Negative-path coverage is not closed: 80 complete, 42 partial, 30 mapped-not-exhaustive, 0 not-evidenced. Callback-only FederateInternalError rows are now excluded from actionable debt.

Python reference is complete-actionable and hosted Python is positive-path passing, CERTI time slices are now verified in this session, but Pitch and the remaining vendor surface are still only partially matrixed.
Lookahead is now explicit in the reference and hosted matrices, and CERTI/Pitch lookahead coverage is being widened into a dedicated backend matrix.
Exchange, synchronization, and ownership slices are now matrixed across Python, hosted Python, Java shim, and CERTI, but negotiated ownership still includes explicit vendor divergences.
Real CERTI time-query/FQR and exchange slices were rerun cleanly in this session, with logical-time type fidelity preserved across native and Java-facade paths.
Plain ownership passed in this session, but negotiated ownership failed across native and Java-facade CERTI paths.

Pitch now has a dedicated positive-path/backend-divergence matrix, but it remains incomplete and negotiated ownership is still vendor-divergent. The requirement disposition audit now exposes every unclassified Pitch row by clause.

A dedicated Section 10 backend matrix now exists, with deep Python reference coverage, executed Java-shim support coverage, transport-backed hosted partial coverage, and smoke-gated CERTI/Pitch wrappers, but the remaining Section 10 slices still need broader parity depth.
